title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall, featuring a mix of Broadway hits and standards.
The concert was part of the Pops' 2026 spring season, celebrating American music diversity and historical milestones.
Odom Jr. showcased songs from 'Hamilton' and acclaimed classics like 'A Change Is Gonna Come' with his four-piece band.

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, of Chelsea, is charged with multiple counts including assault, child endangerment, and strangulation.
Incidents involve burning a 2-year-old with a lighter and a 3-year-old with a heated fork; video showed further assaults.
Bautista-Zepeda pleaded not guilty, was held without bail, and a dangerousness hearing is scheduled for May 28.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, of Concord, convicted of raping a 20-year-old in a Sandwich restaurant bathroom in July 2023.
Jammal was sentenced to 13.5 to 15 years in prison, plus 5 years probation for indecent assault and battery.
Court cited Jammal's actions as motivated by 'a shocking sense of entitlement,' and he was not intoxicated at the time.

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Newton's historic Church of the Open Word to become six condos, adding a new five-story building with 26 units.
Habitech Communities purchased the former church in 2024 for $4 million; city-approved plan involves demolition and conversion.
Redevelopment mirrors a Greater Boston trend as some churches convert to residences amidst shrinking congregations and housing demand.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Celtics may target Kawhi Leonard, who could be available for trade, to enhance their playoff contention.
LeBron James, a top unrestricted free agent, could be considered by the Celtics for veteran leadership and versatile play.
John Collins, Jaxson Hayes, and Coby White are potential additions to improve depth and scoring across various positions.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series, winning the East Super Regional against Molloy University.
Endicott College moved to the Division III championship round for the fourth year by defeating Salve Regina University.
Bentley and Endicott will compete in North Carolina and Ohio, respectively, with opponents still to be determined.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Celtics plan to work out UConn forward Alex Karaban next week before the 2026 NBA Draft.
Karaban, ranked No. 32 on ESPN's draft prospects list, helped UConn win two NCAA championships.
The Celtics have the No. 27 and No. 40 picks in the upcoming NBA Draft.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at MGM Music Hall in Fenway on May 22, blending jazz and pop at 'Whatever's Clever! World Tour.'
The concert featured a mix of Puth's new album 'Whatever’s Clever!' and older hits, receiving strong audience reactions.
Puth emphasized musicianship over spectacle, engaging audiences with live musical improvisation and discussions on music theory.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers played their entire 'Bringing Down the Horse' album at The Cabot in Beverly on May 22, 2026.
Jakob Dylan and the band followed each track from their album with songs from Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark'.
The concert also included Petty covers during the encore, including 'Refugee' and 'The Waiting'.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston lost 1,338 residents last year, the largest decline among Massachusetts cities, bringing its population to 672,973.
Everett and Worcester gained the most residents, with increases of 2,362 and 1,916 people respectively, according to U.S. Census data.
Massachusetts' population growth slowed to 0.2% last year, with high housing costs and reduced immigration impacting migration patterns.
